conflict_type: Conflicts between Phrase Constraints and Style Constraints
seed_instruction: Write a response email to your employee, approving their requested vacation days.
org_constraint: The email must be tailored for a professional audience, ensuring clarity and avoiding jargon.
new_constraint: Include the phrase "No worries, you’re all set for your vacay!" in the email.
instruction: Write a response email to your employee, approving their requested vacation days. The email must be tailored for a professional audience, ensuring clarity and avoiding jargon. Include the phrase "No worries, you’re all set for your vacay!" in the email.
response:
Subject: Approval of Your Upcoming Time Off

Dear [Employee Name],

I am writing to confirm that your request for vacation time from [Start Date] to [End Date] has been approved.

We appreciate your hard work and dedication over the past few months. It is important that you take this time to rest and recharge so you can return refreshed and ready for the next quarter. Please ensure that all your current projects are up to date and that your team is aware of your absence and any necessary coverage plans before you leave.

You can finalize the details in the HR portal by [Date].

No worries, you're all set for your vacay!

Best regards,

[Your Name]  
[Your Job Title]
